title: Rails Security, AI, and IBB
summary: For quite a few years the Rails project has been working with the Internet Bug Bounty (IBB). The IBB is an organization that awarded cash to security researchers that reported issues to OSS projects participating in the IBB. For quite a while I wasn’t certain about my feelings toward the program because I felt like cash rewards could incentivize low…

title: Bainbridge Island Mochi Tsuki
summary: Last weekend we took the ferry to Bainbridge Island to see a Mochi Tsuki event. I read about the history of Japanese immigrants on Bainbridge island. I saw people making mochi, a taiko drummer group, and traditional dances. It was a lot of fun, and I learned a lot. Definitely recommend this event!

title: Pixoo64 Ruby Client
summary: I bought a Pixoo64 LED Display to play around with, and I love it! It connects to WiFi and has an on-board HTTP API so you can program it. I made a Ruby client for it that even includes code to convert PNG files to the binary format the sign wants. One cool thing is that the display can be…

title: Monkey Patch Detection in Ruby
summary: My last post detailed one way that CRuby will eliminate some intermediate array allocations when using methods like Array#hash and Array#max. Part of the technique hinges on detecting when someone monkey patches array. Today, I thought we’d dive a little bit in to how CRuby detects and de-optimizes itself when these “important” methods get monkey patched. Monkey Patching Problem The…

title: Eliminating Intermediate Array Allocations
summary: Recently I gave a talk at RailsWorld (hopefully they’ll post the video soon), and part of my presentation was about eliminating allocations in tokenizers. I presented a simple function for measuring allocations: def allocations x = GC.stat(:total_allocated_objects) yield GC.stat(:total_allocated_objects) - x end Everything in Ruby is an object, but not all objects actually make allocations. We can use the above…

title: Bitmap Matrix and Undirected Graphs in Ruby
summary: I’ve been working my way through Engineering a Compiler. I really enjoy the book, but one part has you build an interference graph for doing register allocation via graph coloring.
